title: facility — trait composition
description: Four owned traits. `BelongsToFacility` is the FK trait for downstream models (ResourceBooking today; Session/EventTeam bookings in Wave 3). `HasBookings` / `HasDayPasses` / `HasPasses` are inverse-side conveniences on the Facility model itself, exposing scoped query helpers (`->activeBookings()`, `->overlappingBookings($window)`, `->activePasses()`). Order matters when composed with `BelongsToTenant` / `BelongsToBranch` / `BelongsToRegion` — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context; branch precedes region because the branch FK is the cascade path for region attribution (facilities cascade region through branch per hierarchy.md §2).

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `facility_id` FK. Adds the `facility()` belongsTo relation, a `resolveInFacility(?Facility)` query scope that filters `WHERE facility_id = ?` (falling back to the caller's `Request::activeFacility()` when the arg is omitted), and a `booted()` hook that fills `facility_id = active_facility_id()` on `creating` when the field is null AND the current request carries a resolved active facility. Refuses to save a row whose `facility_id` points at a Facility in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling — the compliance-auditor would catch a bad schema, this catches a bad write).
usedBy: facility::ResourceBooking (required facility_id — every booking anchored to exactly one Facility)
sports::Session (Wave 3 — practices book a Facility for a window)
sports::EventBooking (Wave 3 — tournaments reserve N contiguous slots)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToFacility` in the trait list so the tenant scope resolves first.
`BelongsToBranch` is implied by the facility — do NOT compose `BelongsToBranch` on models that already carry `BelongsToFacility` (the branch cascades through facility → branch_id in queries).
The `booted()` fill hook is off by default when the consumer's create-flow always specifies facility_id explicitly (ResourceBooking). Turn on per-model via `bool $fillFacilityOnCreating = true`.

owned.description: Composed onto the Facility model itself. Adds `->bookings()` (hasMany over ResourceBooking), `->activeBookings()` (scope filter status IN ['pending', 'confirmed']), `->futureBookings()` (scope filter starts_at > now()), `->overlappingBookings(Carbon $starts, Carbon $ends)` (index-driven overlap query using the covering index on (facility_id, starts_at, ends_at)). The overlap query is the read side of the write-time SERIALIZABLE overlap-detection observer — same predicate, same index.

Composed onto the Branch model (NOT the Facility — day passes are branch-scoped, not facility-scoped). Adds `->dayPasses()`, `->dayPassesOnDate(Carbon $date)` (scope filter valid_on_date = ?), `->activeDayPasses()` (scope filter valid_on_date >= today()). Not composed on Facility because a walk-in visitor buys a day pass to the *branch* (they can use any facility their pass tier grants).

Composed onto the Membership model (Wave 4 — placeholder relation today) AND the Facility model. On Membership: `->passes()` (hasMany over Pass). On Facility: `->passes()` (hasMany filtered on facility_id = self.id OR facility_id IS NULL AND branch_id = self.branch_id — passes that are facility-specific OR any-facility-in-branch). The dual-composition is why the trait is one abstraction: same relation shape, different scoping predicate.
usedBy: finance::Membership (Wave 4 — passes issued against this contract)

framework.reason: Facility, ResourceBooking, Pass. NOT DayPass — day_passes are financial records with no soft-delete (7-year hard retention).

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

branch.reason: Facility, DayPass, Pass all carry branch_id directly. ResourceBooking does NOT — it cascades through facility.branch_id.

audit.reason: Every write to facilities / resource_bookings / day_passes / passes is compliance-relevant (financial-record retention 7 years).

activity.reason: Booking lifecycle transitions + pass credit consumption feed the staff-visible activity feed. NOT on DayPass — POS-issued rows are high-volume + already covered by receipt_number.

facility.notes: Order matters: BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. HasSlug generates the (tenant_id, branch_id)-unique slug on create. HasBookings + HasPasses are inverse-side conveniences. NO BelongsToOrganization / BelongsToRegion — facilities cascade both through branch (hierarchy.md §2 + tenancy-columns.md §5).

resource_booking.notes: NO BelongsToBranch trait — the branch cascades through facility. Bookings inherit tenant/branch/region through the facility chain. Auditable applies because financial retention requires 7-year audit trail on every state transition.

day_pass.notes: NO SoftDeletes — day passes are financial records (7-year hard retention). NO HasActivityLog — POS volume is too high, and every row already has a receipt_number that fills the audit-trail role. Auditable still applies for compliance retention of the payment_method + price_amount_cents fields.

pass.notes: SoftDeletes for the revoke path (soft-delete on revoke, hard-purge after 7-year retention). Auditable is critical — every used_credits increment is a compliance-relevant financial mutation.
